audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as the wisdom poem: mining imagery, hidden depths, wisdom not found by human industry or wealth, Abaddon/Death testimony, and fear of the Lord as wisdom.

verses.restored: Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold that they refine.
Iron is taken from dust, and copper is smelted from stone.
A human puts an end to darkness and searches out to the farthest limit the stone of deep gloom and shadow.
He breaks open a shaft far from where people dwell, forgotten by the foot; they hang and sway far from mortals.
As for the earth, from it comes bread, but underneath it is overturned as by fire.
Its stones are the place of sapphire, and it has dust of gold.
No bird of prey knows that path, and the falcon’s eye has not seen it.
The proud beasts have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
He sends his hand against the flint; he overturns mountains from the root.
He cuts channels in the rocks, and his eye sees every precious thing.
He binds the streams so they do not drip, and what is hidden he brings out to light.
But wisdom, from where shall it be found? And where is the place of understanding?
A mortal does not know its value, and it is not found in the land of the living.
The Deep says, It is not in me; and the Sea says, It is not with me.
Pure gold cannot be given for it, nor can silver be weighed as its price.
It cannot be valued with gold of Ophir, with precious onyx or sapphire.
Gold and glass cannot equal it, nor can it be exchanged for vessels of fine gold.
Coral and crystal shall not be mentioned, and the price of wisdom is above pearls.
The topaz of Cush cannot equal it; it cannot be valued with pure gold.
Wisdom, then, from where does it come? And where is the place of understanding?
It is hidden from the eyes of all living and concealed from the birds of the heavens.
Abaddon and Death say, With our ears we have heard a rumor of it.
The Divine understands its way, and he knows its place.
For he looks to the ends of the earth and sees under all the heavens.
When he gave weight to the wind and measured the waters by measure,
when he made a decree for the rain and a way for the thunderbolt,
then he saw it and recounted it; he established it and also searched it out.
And he said to the human, Look, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and turning away from evil is understanding.
